picks.title: Homelanding
picks.blurb: A free summer festival on the waterfront Marineterrein — food, music, a swim spot and design across the old naval yard.

picks.title: Bruno Mars – The Romantic Tour
picks.blurb: GRAMMY-winning pop sensation brings his first full stadium tour to Amsterdam, with DJ Pee Wee and Victoria Monét.

picks.title: Radio Radio Open
picks.blurb: DJ sets, live radio broadcasts, and special World Pride events at relaxed gathering spot.

picks.title: Rembrandt & Life
picks.blurb: Intimate exhibition on Rembrandt's techniques & life. Hands-on masterclasses reveal his artistic secrets.

picks.title: Love on the Canals – Canal Museum Exhibition
picks.blurb: 30 years of Pride Canal Parade history told through photos, art, personal stories. Opening weekend live.
picks.source: I amsterdam · Your Little Black Book, I amsterdam

picks.title: Hortus Botanicus
picks.blurb: One of the world’s oldest botanical gardens — a butterfly greenhouse and three-climate glasshouse to wander.
picks.source: De Hortus · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: World Press Photo 2026
picks.blurb: International photography's best: 45 winning images from 25 countries. Powerful visual journalism on display.
picks.why: Open both days; major summer exhibition
picks.source: I amsterdam · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: Foodhallen
picks.blurb: An indoor food hall in a converted tram depot — 20-odd stalls, long tables and two bars under one roof.
picks.why: Indoor · grazing for a crowd · weather-proof

picks.title: Wynand Fockink — jenever tasting
picks.blurb: A 1679 tasting room behind Dam Square pouring its own jenevers and liqueurs — you bend to the brimful glass.
picks.why: Historic · indoor · made for a downpour

picks.title: Amsterdam Weekend Guide (3–5 July)
picks.blurb: Official curated guide to Amsterdam's best happenings this exact weekend from local editors and critics.
picks.why: Hand-picked events for this specific weekend

picks.blurb:

picks.blurb:

picks.blurb:
Museum for street art and graffiti

picks.blurb:
Monthly last Sunday dance afternoon

picks.title: Boom Chicago Comedy Festival
picks.blurb: Sixth annual festival with stand-up, improv, sketch. World-class comedians, workshops for all levels.

picks.title: Cabaret at the End of the World
picks.blurb: 100+ singers and dancers celebrate queer resistance with hits by Queen, Stromae, Troye Sivan. Campy, defiant cabaret.

picks.title: Jazz @ H'ART Museum
picks.blurb:
Wonderful jazz in a summer setting
 Jazz lovers can count on a varied program performed by talents from the Amsterdam Conservatory. From energetic saxophone set

picks.title: Julidans Contemporary Dance Festival
picks.blurb: International dance festival showcasing experimental choreography and world-class performers indoors and outdoors.
picks.why: Avant-garde dance, global choreographers, multiple venues

picks.title: Exhibition: the ESSENCE
picks.venue: STRAAT Museum – Street art en graffiti in Amsterdam
picks.blurb:
Core elements of graffiti
 The exhibition focuses on the core elements of graffiti: names, tags, lines and repetition as the building blocks of a visual system.

picks.blurb:
Solo presentations

picks.blurb:

picks.title: Kaboom X Melkweg Cinema
picks.blurb:
Anime in the Milky Way
 Kaboom regularly organizes an anime night at the Melkweg Cinema, so you can once again enjoy the best anime on the big screen! Keep an e

picks.title: Live at Amsterdamse Bos
picks.blurb:
Live in the Amsterdamse Bos

picks.title: Amsterdam - 10 jaar in art prints
picks.blurb: Hilly van Eerten always uses her own photos of the city Amsterdam she loves so much when making her art prints. These photos are only intended as basic material

picks.title: Lunchtime organ concerts at Waterlooplein
picks.blurb:

picks.title: R&B, Hip Hop, Latin & Caribbean Vibes
picks.blurb:
Nightlife
 This evening offers a mix of musical styles. You will experience an atmosphere of old-school R B, hip-hop, and Latin American and Caribbean influence

picks.title: Summer lunchconcerts on the Duyschot organ
picks.blurb:
A musical lunch break
 Admission is free, as are the seats; the Westerkerk would be very grateful for your contribution to the collection afterwards.
 Evan Boge

picks.title: Julidans
picks.venue: Multiple venues (ITA, Theater Bellevue, Vondelpark, others)
picks.blurb: International contemporary dance festival with 26 productions across 9 venues. World-class choreography and experimental work.
picks.why: 36th edition, leading edge dance from global artists

picks.title: Pure Markt
picks.blurb:
Outing for the whole family
 Get your groceries for home, take a seat on one of the terraces to taste the offer in one of the most beautiful parks in Amsterdam,

picks.title: Festival TREK
picks.blurb: Netherlands' largest travelling food festival transforms Amstelpark into open-air restaurant with food trucks, live music, theatre & dozens of international cui
picks.why: Free, all-ages, festival atmosphere with diverse street food

picks.title: Amsterdam Gay Men's Chorus
picks.blurb: 100+ singers and dancers from Amsterdam's LGBTQ+ community perform Queen, Stromae, Troye Sivan, Britney Spears hits.

picks.title: Spinoza
picks.blurb:
Amsterdam, 17th century. 
 A city that sees itself as a safe haven. 
 A Portuguese-Jewish refugee community. 
 Three children. One family. 
 About Spinoza
 Bent

picks.title: TREK Food Festival Amsterdam
picks.blurb: Food trucks, street food, live music, theatre, and cabaret transform the park into an open-air restaurant.

picks.blurb:
A science fiction horror about paranoia and betrayal
 In John Carpenter's The Thing (1982), a group of American scientists at an isolated research station in th

picks.title: Cheeky Monday
picks.blurb:
Always a cool line-up!
 The Amsterdam crew runs one of the longest running club nights in Europe here, and is known worldwide for its great atmosphere, thanks i

picks.title: Museum Market
picks.blurb:
Art, craft and design
 Museum Market takes place every month, every 3rd Sunday of the month (in October the 4th Sunday of the month). Between the trees near the

picks.title: Techno Tuesday
picks.blurb:
Line up
 If you want to know more, check the Melkweg website .

picks.title: Vunzige Deuntjes Festival
picks.blurb: 20,000 visitors, 6+ stages with live acts and DJs. Dancehall, hip hop, house, disco. Food markets, dance battles, karaoke.
picks.why: Urban culture festival, diverse stages spread through nature

picks.title: The Maker Market
picks.blurb:
New editions, discover new creators!
 The Maker Market takes place monthly at De Hallen in Amsterdam-West, a vibrant hub of craftsmanship, design, and inspirati

picks.title: Art Amsterdam Spui
picks.blurb:
Discover art that truly touches you
 At Art Amsterdam Spui, artists present and sell their work themselves — straight from the studio and in direct contact with

picks.title: BOPS queer. pop. party
picks.blurb:
BOPS queer. pop. party
 Lineup: Resident DJs TMORGZ and Absoluut + various guest DJs
 BOPS is a space for the queer community and allies to dance the night away

picks.title: Undivulged All Night Long
picks.blurb:
What to expect?
 Long sets packed with surprises, wild builds, and genre-blending energy from best friends who produce it all. Raw journeys start groovy and ram

picks.title: De Roode Remise Singing Session
picks.blurb:
De Roode Remise Singing Session
 The core singers know each other from the famous Backroom Sessions at Mulligan's Irish Bar, but everyone, experienced or inexpe

picks.title: Lunchconcerten in de Westerkerk
picks.blurb:
Lunch concerts 
 For many years, the Westerkerk has hosted weekly lunch concerts in which the Duyschot organ excels under the hands of young top talents. Baroqu

picks.title: Sauna Deco — Art Deco baths
picks.blurb: A 1920s Art Deco bathhouse (salvaged from a Paris department store) with saunas, steam and a quiet tea room.
picks.why: Warm · restorative · the ultimate cold-and-wet antidote

picks.title: As
picks.blurb: Wood-fired, ingredient-led cooking and natural wine in a striking round garden pavilion.

picks.title: BAK
picks.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.

picks.title: Choux
picks.blurb: Inventive, veg-forward small plates and natural wine in a raw red-brick space near the IJ.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: RIJKS®
picks.blurb: Joris Bijdendijk’s Michelin-starred dining room beside the Rijksmuseum — the “cuisine of the Low Countries”, done with flair.

picks.title: X Bank
picks.blurb: A concept store in a former bank showing 180+ Dutch fashion, design and art labels — couture to hand-thrown ceramics.
picks.why: Best of Dutch design · one room

picks.title: Rijsel
picks.blurb: Lille-inspired French cooking in a converted school — that rotisserie chicken is the order.

picks.title: Bar Fisk
picks.blurb: Sun-soaked Mediterranean seafood and sharing plates in De Pijp.

picks.title: FC Hyena
picks.blurb: An arthouse cinema with a wine bar and a small kitchen, right on the IJ in Noord.

picks.title: Haarlem — the easy escape
picks.blurb: A calmer historic city 15 min away — Grote Markt, the St-Bavo organ, a Saturday market and café-lined square.

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.

picks.title: Utrecht
picks.blurb: Amsterdam’s quieter little sister — wharf-side canals, the soaring Dom Tower and a real café culture, minus the crowds.

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: IJ-Hallen
picks.blurb: Europe’s biggest flea market — 750 stalls across two NDSM shipyard warehouses, a proper treasure-hunt of vintage and junk.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Muiderslot
picks.blurb: A moated medieval castle on the IJmeer — turrets, falconry and a walled garden, the best-preserved fortress in the country.

picks.title: Dappermarkt
picks.blurb: A buzzing, multicultural Oost street market — spices, textiles and world food at honest neighbourhood prices.

picks.title: Bloemenmarkt
picks.blurb: The world’s only floating flower market — barges of tulips, bulbs and seeds moored along the Singel since 1862.

picks.title: Ten Katemarkt
picks.blurb: A proper everyday neighbourhood market off the tourist track — heaped produce, street food and Oud-West locals doing the shop.

picks.title: Marken & Volendam
picks.blurb: Two former Zuiderzee fishing villages — wooden houses, working harbours and smoked eel, an easy half-day from Centraal.

picks.title: Zandvoort Beach
picks.blurb: The nearest North Sea beach — wide sands, beach clubs and a direct train from Centraal; in summer half the city decamps here.

picks.title: Albert Cuypmarkt
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ biggest street market — 250-odd stalls of stroopwafels, cheese, fabric and flowers the length of De Pijp.

picks.title: Lindengrachtmarkt
picks.blurb: The Jordaan’s beloved Saturday market — farm cheese, flowers and Amsterdam’s best market-stall fish, all down a leafy street.

picks.title: Kinderdijk Windmills
picks.blurb: Nineteen 18th-century windmills lined along the polders — the postcard Dutch landscape, a UNESCO site you can walk or cycle.

picks.title: Throwback AMSTERDAM - Back to 80s, 90s & 00s
picks.blurb: Club night at Het Sieraad.

picks.title: BiBi presents Sam Alfred, The Trip
picks.blurb: Sam Alfred, The Trip, PHIA, IN2POL at Shelter Amsterdam.
